metadata.working_title: An artificial intelligence accelerated virtual screening platform for drug discovery

abstract_sketch.problem: Structure-based virtual screening against multi-billion compound libraries is limited by docking accuracy and computational cost, and open-source active-learning platforms with accurate physics-based force fields are lacking.
abstract_sketch.gap: Leading physics-based docking programs are proprietary, free programs trail them in accuracy, deep-learning docking methods generalize poorly, and existing open-source pipelines do not combine active learning with flexible-receptor physics-based docking for ultra-large libraries.
abstract_sketch.approach: We developed RosettaGenFF-VS and the RosettaVS protocol (VSX fast mode, VSH flexible-receptor mode) inside Rosetta GALigandDock, and integrated them into an open-source AI-accelerated virtual screening (OpenVS) platform that uses active learning to triage billions of compounds.
abstract_sketch.key_results: On CASF2016 screening power, RosettaGenFF-VS achieves EF1%=16.72 versus 11.9 for the second-best method; on DUD, RosettaVS outperforms the second-best baseline by a factor of two at 0.5/1.0% ROC enrichment; screening 5.5B Enamine-REAL compounds against KLHDC2 and 4.1B ZINC22 compounds against NaV1.7 each completed in under seven days on a 3000-CPU / 1 RTX2080 GPU HPC cluster, yielding seven novel KLHDC2 binders (14% hit rate) and four novel NaV1.7 binders (44.4% hit rate) with single-digit micromolar affinities; a 2.0 A X-ray structure of KLHDC2 with compound 29 (IC50 ~3 uM) validates the predicted binding pose.
abstract_sketch.impact: Provides an accurate, open-source platform for ultra-large virtual screens that can discover novel chemotypes for challenging targets and supports structure-guided lead optimization.

sections.title: Introduction
content_bullets: Structure-based virtual screening drives lead discovery, and multi-billion compound libraries now enable deeper chemical-space exploration.
Ultra-large library screening is time- and cost-prohibitive for physics-based docking; existing speedups include HPC parallelization, active learning, hierarchical screening, and GPU acceleration.

Success depends on the accuracy of ligand docking; Glide and GOLD are accurate but proprietary, Autodock Vina is free but less accurate, and deep-learning docking is better for blind docking and generalizes poorly to novel complexes when the binding site is known.

This work presents RosettaGenFF-VS, the RosettaVS protocol with VSX and VSH modes inside Rosetta GALigandDock, and the OpenVS open-source platform that integrates active learning for scalable screens.
We validate on CASF2016 and DUD benchmarks and apply the platform to two unrelated targets (KLHDC2 E3 ligase, NaV1.7 VSD4) with experimental validation and X-ray crystallography.

sections.title: Background and Related Work
content_bullets: Ultra-large compound libraries (Enamine REAL, ZINC22) and scalable docking platforms.
Active-learning and deep-learning-guided chemical-space exploration for virtual screening.
Physics-based docking programs (Glide, GOLD, Vina) and the Rosetta GALigandDock / RosettaGenFF force field.
Deep-learning scoring functions and their generalization limits; benchmark contamination concerns.
KLHDC2 as a PROTAC E3 platform; NaV1.7 VSD4 pharmacology and fast-inactivation state dependence.

sections.title: Methods
content_bullets: RosettaGenFF-VS extends Rosetta GALigandDock with new atom types, new torsional potentials, improved preprocessing, and an entropy (Delta S) model added to the prior enthalpy (Delta H) model.

RosettaVS protocol provides two modes: VSX (virtual screening express) for fast initial docking and VSH (virtual screening high-precision) with full receptor sidechain flexibility for final re-ranking.
OpenVS open-source platform combines RosettaVS with an active-learning surrogate neural network trained during docking to select promising compounds, and is designed to scale to HPC clusters.
Benchmark protocols: CASF2016 (285 complexes) docking power, screening power (EF at X% cutoff, best-binder success rate at 1/5/10%); DUD (40 targets, >100k molecules) AUC and ROC enrichment.

KLHDC2 campaign: Enamine-REAL (~5.5 billion, 80% synthesis success rate) docked with VSX, top 50,000 re-docked with VSH, top 1000 filtered for solubility, hydrogen-bond completeness, similarity clustering; 54 clustered candidates, 37 selected, 29 synthesized; AlphaLISA displacement assay and BLI confirmation; X-ray crystallography of apo KLHDC2 soaked with C29 to 2.0 A resolution.
KLHDC2 focused screen: substructure search (acetyl-amino-methyl-triazole-acetic acid backbone) against ZINC22 yielded ~381,567 hits; 21 synthesized and re-tested.

NaV1.7 VSD4 campaign: ZINC22 (~4.1 billion) VSX screen (stopped after 7 iterations), top 100,000 re-docked with VSH, clustered, top 1000 filtered, 160 manually examined, 10 selected (Tanimoto < 0.33 to known inhibitors), 9 synthesized; whole-cell patch-clamp electrophysiology on hNaV1.7 stably expressed in HEK-293; selectivity tested against hNaV1.5 and hERG.

subsections.title: Development of an AI accelerated virtual screening platform
content_bullets: RosettaGenFF-VS adds an entropy term to GALigandDock's enthalpy force field and extends atom types / torsions.
RosettaVS provides VSX and VSH modes with VSH adding full sidechain flexibility for final re-ranking.
OpenVS platform uses active-learning surrogate training to avoid docking the entire library.

subsections.title: RosettaVS achieves state-of-the-art virtual screening benchmark performance
content_bullets: CASF2016 docking power: RosettaGenFF-VS achieves leading success rate in top 1/2/3 decoys with <2 A RMSD.
CASF2016 screening power: top 1% enrichment factor EF1%=16.72 for RosettaGenFF-VS versus 11.9 for the second-best method.
CASF2016 screening power subsets: improved performance on polar, shallower and smaller protein pockets.
DUD: RosettaVS leads on AUC and ROC enrichment; outperforms second-best method by a factor of two at 0.5/1.0% ROC enrichment; VSH marginally outperforms VSX via receptor flexibility.

subsections.title: Discovery of novel KLHDC2 ubiquitin ligase binders
content_bullets: Active-learning VSX screen against Enamine-REAL (~5.5B compounds); predicted binding affinity of the top 0.1 percentile improved from -6.81 kcal/mol (iteration 1) to -12.43 kcal/mol (iteration 10); screen stopped after iteration 10.
~6 million compounds (0.11%) were subjected to docking; top 50,000 re-docked with VSH; whole campaign completed within a week on 3000 CPUs + 1 RTX2080 GPU.
Top 1000 filtered, 54 clustered, 37 chosen for synthesis, 29 successfully synthesized.
Compound 29 (C29) is best initial hit with IC50 ~3 uM in AlphaLISA displacement assay, confirmed by BLI.

KLHDC2-C29 co-crystal structure at 2.0 A shows C29 distal carboxyl group contacting Arg236, Arg241 and Ser269; triazole near Tyr163, Trp191, Trp270 with NH...N hydrogen bond; Lys147 hydrogen bond; tert-butylphenyl in auxiliary chamber.

Focused ZINC22 substructure screen (acetyl-amino-methyl-triazole-acetic acid backbone) returned ~381,567 compounds; 21 synthesized, 6 additional hits with single-digit uM IC50; seven low-micromolar KLHDC2 binders total.
Hit rate: 7/29 + 6/21 -> 14% overall hit rate for KLHDC2 (as reported).

subsections.title: Discovery of novel NaV1.7 VSD4 antagonists
content_bullets: Active-learning VSX screen against ZINC22 (~4.1B compounds); top 0.1 percentile predicted affinity improved from -10.8 kcal/mol (iteration 1) to -18.2 kcal/mol (final iteration); stopped after iteration 7.
~4.5 million compounds (0.11%) docked; top 100,000 re-docked with VSH.
Top 100,000 clustered; top 1000 cluster representatives filtered; 160 manually examined; 10 selected (Tanimoto < 0.33 to known ChEMBL inhibitors), 9 successfully synthesized.
Whole-cell patch-clamp on hNaV1.7 in HEK-293: four compounds show IC50 < 10 uM (44.4% hit rate).
Best compound Z8739902234: inactivated-state IC50 = 1.32 uM (95% CI 1.14-1.55), resting-state IC50 = 13.1 uM (12.78-13.45); state-dependent inhibition.
Second-best Z8739905023: inactivated-state IC50 = 2.23 uM (2.14-2.46).

sections.title: Discussion
content_bullets: RosettaGenFF-VS / RosettaVS combine high docking accuracy with efficient sampling, driving strong performance on CASF2016 and DUD.
Unlike many methods, RosettaGenFF-VS improves on polar, shallow and small pockets via better protein-ligand vs intra-ligand energy balance.
Two parallel campaigns (KLHDC2, NaV1.7) each completed in <7 days on modest HPC resources and yielded multiple novel hits with single-digit uM binding.

Future work: GPU acceleration of Rosetta docking, generative AI pose generation, improved active-learning surrogates, deep-learning score functions, template-guided screening using macrocycles or antibody loops.
